TABULA TEMPORARIA — ANNO DOMINI 2026
============================================================
  Golden Number                XIII
  Dominical Letter             D
  Sundays after Epiphany       4
  Sundays after Pentecost      25

  MOVEABLE FEASTS
    Septuagesima Sunday      February 8
    Ash Wednesday            February 25
    Easter (Pascha) Day      April 12
    Ascension Day            May 21
    Pentecost                May 31
    Corpus Christi           June 11
    Advent Sunday            November 29

  EMBER DAYS
    Spring (Lent)            March 4, 6, 7
    Summer (Whitsun)         June 3, 5, 6
    Autumn (Holy Cross)      September 16, 18, 19
    Winter (Advent)          December 16, 18, 19


JANUARY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Thu     Circumcision of Our Lord                   [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This day is set forth a wonderful mystery · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, Mary hath brought forth · No Suff.
  2  Fri  L  Octave Day of St Stephen                   [s]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · Mag. Devout men buried Stephen · No Suff.
  3  Sat     Octave Day of St. John, Apostle & Evangelist [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Holy Name of Jesus) · Mag. I will offer · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of St. John, Apostle & Evangelist ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Octave Day of the Holy Innocents ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  4  Sun     Holy Name of Jesus                         [2cl] w
           Octave Day of the Holy Innocents (s)
           St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Young men and maidens · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of the Holy Innocents ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. I will offer · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of the Holy Innocents ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. The Vigil of the Epiphany ("While all things were in quiet silence")
  5  Mon     The Vigil of the Epiphany                  [sd]  w
           St. Telesphorus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. While all things were in quiet silence · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Telesphorus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Epiphany of Our Lord) · Mag. This day we keep a holiday in honour of… · No Suff.
               Com. The Vigil of the Epiphany ("While all things were in quiet silence")
  6  Tue     EPIPHANY OF OUR LORD                       [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This day is the Church joined unto the Heavenly… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. This day we keep a holiday in honour of… · No Suff.
  7  Wed     Day II within the Octave of the Epiphany   [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. From the east · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. When the wise men saw the star · No Suff.
  8  Thu     Day III within the Octave of the Epiphany  [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Three are the gifts · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Light of light · No Suff.
  9  Fri  L  Day IV within the Octave of the Epiphany   [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. We have seen his star · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Herod inquired · No Suff.
 10  Sat     Day V within the Octave of the Epiphany    [sd]  w
           St. Paul the First Hermit (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Many nations · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Paul the First Hermit ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday within the Octave of Epiphany) · Mag. But Mary · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
 11  Sun     Sunday within the Octave of Epiphany       [sd]  w
           St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Enlighten us, O Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. But Mary · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 12  Mon     Day VII within the Octave of the Epiphany  [sd]  w
           St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. They that despised thee · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Epiphany) · Mag. This day we keep a holiday in honour of… · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
 13  Tue     Octave Day of the Epiphany                 [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This day is the Church joined unto the Heavenly… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. This day we keep a holiday in honour of… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 14  Wed     St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor     [d]   w
           St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Maurus, Abbot) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
 15  Thu     St Maurus, Abbot                           [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. St. Marcellus I of Rome, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 16  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Marcellus I of Rome, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Marcellus I of Rome, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Anthony of Egypt) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
 17  Sat     St. Anthony of Egypt                       [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (II Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St. Anthony of Egypt ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 18  Sun     II Sunday after Epiphany                   [sd]  g
           Chair of St Peter in Rome (gd)
           Commemoration of St Paul (com)
           St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. There was a marriage · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. The wine failing · No Suff.
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 19  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs (com)
           St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs) · Mag. For theirs is the Kingdom of heaven · No Suff.
 20  Tue     Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs   [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Agnes of Rome, Virgin & Martyr) · Mag. The blessed Agnes · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 21  Wed     St. Agnes of Rome, Virgin & Martyr         [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Behold, now I see that · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. The blessed Agnes stood in the midst of the… · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vincent, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 22  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Ss Vincent, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Vincent, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. Ss Vincent, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 23  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Emerentiana, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Emerentiana, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr) · Mag. This man is holy · No Suff.
 24  Sat     St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr                [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (III Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Ananias, go thy way")
               Com. Commemoration of St. Peter ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 25  Sun     III Sunday after Epiphany                  [sd]  g
           Conversion of St. Paul (gd)
           Commemoration of St. Peter (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When Jesus was come down from the mountain · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Ananias, go thy way")
               Com. Commemoration of St. Peter ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. Lord, if Thou wilt · No Suff.
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Ananias, go thy way")
               Com. Commemoration of St. Peter ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 26  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
 27  Tue     St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Cyril of Alexandria, Confessor, Bishop & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 28  Wed     St Cyril of Alexandria, Confessor, Bishop & Doctor [d]   w
           The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 29  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 30  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Martina, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Martina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
 31  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (IV Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")

FEBRUARY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sun     IV Sunday after Epiphany                   [sd]  g
           St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr (d)
           St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When Jesus was entered into a ship · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Purification of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. They offered for Him · No Suff.
               Com. IV Sunday after Epiphany ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  2  Mon     Purification of the Blessed Virgin Mary    [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. They offered for Him · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. They offered for Him · No Suff.
  3  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Blaise, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Blaise, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Blaise, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  4  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Joseph of Aleppo, Martyr (com)
           The New Martyrs of Russia (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Joseph of Aleppo, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. The New Martyrs of Russia ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Agatha) · Mag. The blessed Agatha · No Suff.
  5  Thu     St. Agatha                                 [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The heathen multitude made haste · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Upon Him Who hath vouchsafed · No Suff.
               Com. St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Photius, Bishop and Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  6  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           St Photius, Bishop and Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Photius, Bishop and Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St Romuald, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
  7  Sat     Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany      [sd]  g
           St Romuald, Abbot (d)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Romuald, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Septuagesima Sunday) · Mag. The Lord said unto Adam · Suff.
               Com. Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  8  Sun     Septuagesima Sunday                        [2cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Praise ye the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. The householder saith unto his labourers · Suff.
               Com. St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
  9  Mon     Feria                                            v
           St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Scholastica, Virgin) · Mag. Come, Bride of Christ, and take the everlasting crown · No Suff.
 10  Tue     St Scholastica, Virgin                     [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Septuagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Come, Bride of Christ, and take the everlasting crown · No Suff.
               Com. St Gregory II, Pope & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 11  Wed     Feria                                            v
           St Gregory II, Pope & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory II, Pope & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Take that thine is · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory II, Pope & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 12  Thu     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Is it not lawful for me · Suff.
 13  Fri  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
 14  Sat     Feria                                            v
           St Valentine, Priest & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Valentine, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Sexagesima Sunday) · Mag. The Lord said unto Noah · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 15  Sun     Sexagesima Sunday                          [2cl] v
           Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Praise ye the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Jesus said to His disciples · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 16  Mon     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. If ye would be truly honourable · Suff.
 17  Tue     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The seed is the word of God · Suff.
 18  Wed     Feria                                            v
           St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. But that on the good ground · Suff.
               Com. St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 19  Thu     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 20  Fri  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
 21  Sat     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Quinquagesima Sunday) · Mag. The father of our faith · Suff.
 22  Sun     Quinquagesima Sunday                       [2cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Praise ye the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Chair of St. Peter at Antioch) · Mag. Thou art the Shepherd · No Suff.
               Com. Quinquagesima Sunday ("The father of our faith")
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 23  Mon     Chair of St. Peter at Antioch              [2cl] w
           St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Commemoration of St Paul (com)
           Vigil of St. Matthias (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Monday after Quinquagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Vigil of St. Matthias ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. While he was supreme Pontiff · No Suff.
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Vigil of St. Matthias ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Matthias, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
 24  Tue     St. Matthias, Apostle                      [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Quinquagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be valiant · No Suff.
               Com. Ash Wednesday ("Turn ye even to me")
 25  Wed  L§ ASH WEDNESDAY                              [1cl] v
           St Walburga of Heidenheim, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. When ye fast · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Walburga of Heidenheim, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven · Suff.
               Com. St Walburga of Heidenheim, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 26  Thu  L§ Feria                                            v
           St. Alexander of Alexandria, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Alexander of Alexandria, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St. Raphael of Brooklyn) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
 27  Fri  L§ St. Raphael of Brooklyn                    [gd]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Friday after Quinquagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. The Lord loved him · No Suff.
 28  Sat  L§ Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. For they seek me · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (I Sunday in Lent) · Mag. Then shalt thou call · Suff.
               Com. St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")

MARCH
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sun  L  I SUNDAY IN LENT                           [1cl] v
           St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus was led up of the Spirit into the… · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Then shalt thou call · Suff.
               Com. St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St Chad, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  2  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent I                              v
           St Chad, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Come, ye blessed of My Father · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Chad, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. As long as you did it to one · Suff.
               Com. St Chad, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  3  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent I                             v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus went into · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. For it is written · Suff.
  4  Wed  L§ Lent Ember Wednesday                       [sd]  v
           St Lucius I, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. This evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Lucius I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. As Jonas was three days and three nights in… · Suff.
               Com. St Lucius I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  5  Thu  L§ Thursday after Lent I                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And Jesus went from thence · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs) · Mag. For theirs is the Kingdom of heaven · No Suff.
               Com. Lent Ember Friday ("The Angel of the Lord")
  6  Fri  L§ St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs           [d]   v
           Lent Ember Friday (sd)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Lent Ember Friday ("The Angel of the Lord")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. In heaven do rejoice the souls of the Saints · No Suff.
               Com. Lent Ember Friday ("The Angel of the Lord")
               Com. Lent Ember Saturday ("Jesus took His disciples")
  7  Sat  L§ Lent Ember Saturday                        [sd]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus took His disciples · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (II Sunday in Lent) · Mag. Tell the vision that ye have seen to no… · Suff.
               Com. Lent Ember Saturday ("Jesus took His disciples")
  8  Sun  L  II SUNDAY IN LENT                          [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus took His disciples · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Tell the vision that ye have seen to no… · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
  9  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent II                             v
           St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The beginning · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He That sent Me is with Me · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
 10  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent II                            v
           The Forty Holy Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. For One is your Master · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. The Forty Holy Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. And all ye are brethren · Suff.
               Com. The Forty Holy Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 11  Wed  L§ Wednesday after Lent II                          v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Behold, we go up to Jerusalem · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Gregory the Great) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
 12  Thu  L§ St. Gregory the Great                      [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent II ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
 13  Fri  L§ Friday after Lent II                             v
           Lauds   v · Ben. He will miserably destroy · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. When they sought · Suff.
 14  Sat  L§ Saturday after Lent II                           v
           Lauds   v · Ben. I will arise and go to my father · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (III Sunday in Lent) · Mag. But the father said · Suff.
 15  Sun  L  III SUNDAY IN LENT                         [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. When a strong man armed keepeth his palace · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. A certain woman of the company lifed up her… · Suff.
 16  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent III                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Amen I say unto you · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St. Patrick) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. St Joseph of Arimathea, C ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 17  Tue  L§ St. Patrick                                [d]   v
           St Joseph of Arimathea, C (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Lent III ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
               Com. St Joseph of Arimathea, C ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. St. Patrick ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St Edward, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 18  Wed  L§ St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   v
           St Edward, King & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Wednesday after Lent III ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
               Com. St Edward, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. Joseph, being raised · No Suff.
               Com. St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
 19  Thu  L§ St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Jesus went down with them · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent III ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. But while he thought on these things, behold, the… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 20  Fri  L§ St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor           [d]   v

               Com. Friday after Lent III ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Benedict) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 21  Sat  L§ St. Benedict                               [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday after Lent III ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers p · I fol. (IV Sunday in Lent) · Mag. Woman, hath no man condemned thee? · No Suff.
               Com. St. Benedict ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 22  Sun  L  IV SUNDAY IN LENT                          [1cl] p
           Lauds   p · Ben. When Jesus lifted up His Eyes · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers p · II prec. · Mag. Woman, hath no man condemned thee? · Suff.
 23  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent IV                             v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St. Gabriel the Archangel) · Mag. The Angel Gabriel · No Suff.

               Com. Tuesday after Lent IV ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Annunciation of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. The Angel Gabriel said unto Mary: Hail, thou that… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gabriel the Archangel ("The Angel Gabriel was sent")
 25  Wed  L§ ANNUNCIATION OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY    [1cl] w

               Com. Wednesday after Lent IV ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Angel Gabriel said unto Mary: Hail, thou that… · No Suff.
 26  Thu  L§ Thursday after Lent IV                           v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus went · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (St. John Damascene, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.

               Com. Friday after Lent IV ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
 28  Sat  L§ Saturday after Lent IV                           v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Sunday of the Passion) · Mag. I am One · No Suff.

           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day · No Suff.
               Com. St John Climacus, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")

           St John Climacus, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. In the last day, that great day of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St John Climacus, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces

               Com. St John Climacus, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 31  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Passion Sunday                     v
           St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor (com)

               Com. St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Go ye up unto this Feast · No Suff.
               Com. St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")

APRIL
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Wed  L§ Wednesday after Passion Sunday                   v
           Lauds   v · Ben. My sheep hear My voice · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Many good works have I wrought · No Suff.
  2  Thu  L§ Thursday after Passion Sunday                    v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Master saith: My time is at hand · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Our Lady of Sorrows) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
  3  Fri  L§ Our Lady of Sorrows                        [gd]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
  4  Sat  L§ St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday after Passion Sunday ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Palm Sunday) · Mag. It is written: I will smite the Shepherd · No Suff.
               Com. St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
  5  Sun  L  PALM SUNDAY                                [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Much people that were come to the Feast, cried · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. It is written: I will smite the Shepherd · No Suff.
               Com. Holy Monday ("And now, O Father, glorify Thou Me")
  6  Mon  L§ HOLY MONDAY                                [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And now, O Father, glorify Thou Me · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Thou couldest have no power · No Suff.
  7  Tue  L§ HOLY TUESDAY                               [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Now, before the Feast of the Passover, as Jesus… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. I have power · No Suff.
  8  Wed  L§ HOLY WEDNESDAY                             [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Simon, do you sleep? · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The handmaid said to Peter · No Suff.
  9  Thu  L§ HOLY THURSDAY                              [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Now he that betrayed Him · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And, as they were eating · No Suff.
               Com. Good Friday ("God spared not His Own Son")
 10  Fri  L§ GOOD FRIDAY                                [1cl] b
           Lauds   b · Ben. They set up over his head his accusation written · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers b · II prec. · Mag. When He had received the vinegar · No Suff.
               Com. Holy Saturday ("O all ye that pass by")
 11  Sat  L§ HOLY SATURDAY                              [1cl] w
           St Leo the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Lauds   w · Ben. There were women sitting over against the sepulchre · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Leo the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday of the Resurrection) · Mag. And looking · No Suff.
               Com. Holy Saturday ("O all ye that pass by")
 12  Sun     SUNDAY OF THE RESURRECTION                 [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. And the angel answering · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And looking · No Suff.
               Com. Easter Monday ("And the angel answering")
 13  Mon     EASTER MONDAY                              [1cl] w
           St Hermengild, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. While they walked · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermengild, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. What manner of communications are these · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermengild, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Easter Tuesday ("Jesus stood in the midst of His disciples")
 14  Tue     EASTER TUESDAY                             [1cl] w
           St Justin, Martyr (com)
           St. Tiburtius, Valerian & Maximus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Jesus stood in the midst of His disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Justin, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Tiburtius, Valerian & Maximus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Behold My Hands · No Suff.
               Com. St Justin, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Tiburtius, Valerian & Maximus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of Easter ("And in the end of the sabbath")
 15  Wed     DAY IV WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER         [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Cast the net on the right side of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Jesus saith · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Easter ("And in the end of the sabbath")
 16  Thu     DAY V WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER          [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Mary stood without at the Sepulchre weeping · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. They have taken away my Lord · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Easter ("And in the end of the sabbath")
 17  Fri     DAY VI WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER         [1cl] w
           St Anicetus, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. When the eleven disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Anicetus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All power is given unto Me · No Suff.
               Com. St Anicetus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Easter ("And in the end of the sabbath")
 18  Sat     DAY VII WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER        [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. They ran both together · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Low Sunday (Octave of Easter)) · Mag. Now when it was late · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Easter ("And in the end of the sabbath")
 19  Sun     LOW SUNDAY (OCTAVE OF EASTER)              [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Tikhon of Moscow) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. Low Sunday (Octave of Easter) ("Now when it was late")
 20  Mon     ST. TIKHON OF MOSCOW                       [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Lord loved him · No Suff.
 21  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I go before you into Galilee · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Reach hither thine hand · No Suff.
 22  Wed     Feria                                            w
           Ss. Soter & Caius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am the True Vine · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Soter & Caius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. George) · Mag. Eternal light will shine · No Suff.

           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Saints and just · No Suff.

           Day IV within the Octave of St. George (sd)

           Hours   No Preces

               Com. St. Cletus & Marcellinus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")

           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · Mag. Saints and just · No Suff.
