               Com. Day VII within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
 29  Wed     SOLEMNITY OF ST. JOSEPH, SPOUSE OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY [1cl] w
           Day VII within the Octave of St. George (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
               Com. Octave Day of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
 30  Thu     Octave Day of St. George                   [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. Philip and James, Apostles) · Mag. Let not your heart be troubled · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")

MAY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Fri  L  Ss. Philip and James, Apostles             [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the Way · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If ye love Me · No Suff.
               Com. St. Athanasius ("Behold an high priest")
  2  Sat     St. Athanasius                             [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Finding of the Holy Cross) · Mag. By thy Cross' holy sign · No Suff.
               Com. St. Athanasius ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  3  Sun     Finding of the Holy Cross                  [2cl] r
           Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- (com)
           III Sunday after Easter (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thou art higher · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. By thy Cross' holy sign · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  4  Mon     Feria                                            w
           St. Monica, Widow (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Monica, Widow ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · No Suff.
               Com. St. Monica, Widow ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
  5  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. John before the Latin Gate) · Mag. The blessed Apostle John · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
  6  Wed     St. John before the Latin Gate             [gd]  r
           Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Saints and just · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
  7  Thu     Feria                                            w
           St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And He went in with them · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Apparition of St. Michael the Archangel) · Mag. While as John was gazing · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  8  Fri  L  Apparition of St. Michael the Archangel    [gd]  w
           St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. There was silence · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O ye Angels and Archangels · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
  9  Sat     St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (IV Sunday after Easter) · Mag. I go My way to Him That sent Me · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 10  Sun     IV Sunday after Easter                     [sd]  w
           Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I go My way to Him That sent Me · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. I go My way to Him That sent Me · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 11  Mon     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Did not our heart burn within us, at the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I tell you the truth · No Suff.
 12  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Peace be unto you · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. When the Comforter, the Spirit of truth, is come · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 13  Wed     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. A spirit hath not flesh and bones · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I have yet many things to say unto you… · No Suff.
 14  Thu     Feria                                            w
           St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The disciples gave the Lord a piece · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. For He shall not speak of Himself · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 15  Fri  L  Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. These are the words · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
 16  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (V Sunday after Easter) · Mag. Hitherto · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 17  Sun     V Sunday after Easter                      [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Hitherto · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Ask, and ye shall receive · No Suff.
               Com. St Venantius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 18  Mon     St Venantius, Martyr                       [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Saints and just · No Suff.
               Com. St Pudentiana, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 19  Tue     Feria                                            w
           St Pudentiana, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Christ ought to have suffered · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Pudentiana, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I came forth from the Father · No Suff.
               Com. St Pudentiana, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 20  Wed     Vigil of the Ascension                     [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Father, the hour is come · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Ascension of Our Lord) · Mag. O King of glory, Lord of hosts · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of the Ascension ("Father, the hour is come")
 21  Thu     ASCENSION OF OUR LORD                      [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend to my Father · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O King of glory, Lord of hosts · No Suff.
 22  Fri  L  Day II within the Octave of the Ascension  [sd]  w
           St. Romanus, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend to my Father · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Romanus, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O King of glory, Lord of hosts · No Suff.
               Com. St. Romanus, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 23  Sat     Day III within the Octave of the Ascension [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend to my Father · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday within the Octave of the Ascension) · Mag. But when the Comforter is come · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 24  Sun     Sunday within the Octave of the Ascension  [sd]  w
           St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. But when the Comforter is come · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. These things have I told · No Suff.
               Com. St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Urban, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 25  Mon     Day V within the Octave of the Ascension   [sd]  w
           St Urban, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend to my Father · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Urban, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Eleutherius, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
 26  Tue     St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor [d]   w
           St Eleutherius, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Eleutherius, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St John I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
 27  Wed     St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor  [d]   w
           St John I, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St John I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Ascension) · Mag. O King of glory, Lord of hosts · No Suff.
               Com. St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
 28  Thu     Octave Day of the Ascension                [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend to my Father · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O King of glory, Lord of hosts · No Suff.
 29  Fri  L  Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. But when the Comforter is come · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · No Suff.
 30  Sat  L§ VIGIL OF PENTECOST                         [1cl] w
           St Felix I, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Felix I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Whitsunday — Pentecost) · Mag. This day · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St Petronilla, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 31  Sun     WHITSUNDAY — PENTECOST                     [1cl] r
           St Petronilla, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Receive ye the Holy Ghost · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Petronilla, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. This day · No Suff.
               Com. St Petronilla, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")

JUNE
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Mon     DAY II WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST      [1cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. God so loved the world · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If a Man love Me · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
  2  Tue     DAY III WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST     [1cl] r
           Ss. Marcellinus, Peter & Erasmus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the Door · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Marcellinus, Peter & Erasmus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Peace I leave with you · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Marcellinus, Peter & Erasmus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
  3  Wed  L§ DAY IV WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST      [1cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the Living Bread · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. I am the Living Bread Which came down from… · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
  4  Thu     DAY V WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST       [1cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Jesus called His twelve disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. The Comforter · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
  5  Fri  L§ DAY VI WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST      [1cl] r
           St Boniface, Bishop & Martyr (d)
           Lauds   r · Ben. But that ye may know · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. But the Comforter which is the Holy Ghost · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
  6  Sat  L§ DAY VII WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST     [1cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The charity of God · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Trinity Sunday) · Mag. With all our heart · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Pentecost ("When the day of Pentecost was fully come")
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  7  Sun     TRINITY SUNDAY                             [1cl] w
           I Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed be the Holy One · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. With all our heart · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
  8  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Columba of Iona, Abbot) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  9  Tue     St Columba of Iona, Abbot                  [d]   w
           Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 10  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Corpus Christi) · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Barnabas, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
 11  Thu     CORPUS CHRISTI                             [1cl] w
           St. Barnabas, Apostle (gd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Barnabas, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Barnabas, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
 12  Fri  L  Day II within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Ss Basilides, Cyrinus, Nabor & Nazarius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Basilides, Cyrinus, Nabor & Nazarius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Basilides, Cyrinus, Nabor & Nazarius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 13  Sat     Day III within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (II Sunday after Pentecost, within the Octave of Corpus Christi) · Mag. Go out quickly into the streets · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Corpus Christi ("Christ the Lord, being made an High Priest for…")
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. All Saints of Antioch ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 14  Sun     II Sunday after Pentecost, within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (gd)
           All Saints of Antioch (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. A certain man made a great supper · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. All Saints of Antioch ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Go out quickly into the streets · No Suff.
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. All Saints of Antioch ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Corpus Christi ("Christ the Lord, being made an High Priest for…")
               Com. Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 15  Mon     Day V within the Octave of Corpus Christi  [sd]  w
           Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 16  Tue     Day VI within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
 17  Wed     Day VII within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of Corpus Christi) · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Corpus Christi ("Christ the Lord, being made an High Priest for…")
               Com. St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 18  Thu     Octave Day of Corpus Christi               [gd]  w
           St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. To him that overcometh will I give of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Lord, That maketh peace in the borders of… · No Suff.
               Com. St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Ss. Gervase & Protase, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 19  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss. Gervase & Protase, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Gervase & Protase, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
               Com. St Silverius, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 20  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           St Silverius, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Silverius, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (III Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. And they all knew · Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 21  Sun     III Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. What man of you · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. What woman · No Suff.
               Com. St Alban, Protomartyr of England ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Paulinus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 22  Mon     St Alban, Protomartyr of England           [d]   r
           St Paulinus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Paulinus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If any man will come after me · No Suff.
               Com. St Paulinus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Vigil of St John the Baptist ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St. Etheldreda, Queen & Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 23  Tue     Vigil of St John the Baptist               [s]   v
           St. Etheldreda, Queen & Virgin (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Etheldreda, Queen & Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Nativity of St. John the Baptist) · Mag. When Zacharias went · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of St John the Baptist ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 24  Wed     NATIVITY OF ST. JOHN THE BAPTIST           [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. And the mouth of Zacharias was opened · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The child, that unto us is born · No Suff.
 25  Thu     Day II within the Octave of St John Baptist [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. And the mouth of Zacharias was opened · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. John & Paul, Martyrs) · Mag. The righteous stand · No Suff.
               Com. Day II within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
 26  Fri  L  Ss. John & Paul, Martyrs                   [d]   r
           Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ (com)
           Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. These are the holy ones · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. These are two olive trees · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Vigil of Ss Peter & Paul ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 27  Sat     Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist [sd]  w
           Vigil of Ss Peter & Paul (s)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And the mouth of Zacharias was opened · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of Ss Peter & Paul ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (IV Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. So David prevailed · No Suff.
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. St Leo II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
 28  Sun     IV Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           St Leo II, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Day V within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Jesus entered into the ship · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Leo II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. Peter and Paul, Apostles) · Mag. For they shall deliver you · No Suff.
               Com. IV Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
 29  Mon     SS. PETER AND PAUL, APOSTLES               [1cl] r
           Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Thou art Peter · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
 30  Tue     Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle          [gd]  w
           Commemoration of St Peter, Apostle (com)
           Day VII within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Day II within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Commemoration of St Peter, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave of St John Baptist) · Mag. When Zacharias went · No Suff.
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")

JULY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Wed     Octave of St John Baptist                  [gd]  w
           Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And the mouth of Zacharias was opened · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. As soon as · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before Him")
               Com. Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  2  Thu     Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary      [2cl] w
           Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs (com)
           St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Day IV within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. When Elizabeth heard the salutation · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. As soon as · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. St Irenaeus of Lyons, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  3  Fri  L  St Irenaeus of Lyons, Bishop & Martyr      [d]   r
           Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If any man will come after me · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  4  Sat     Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul [sd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (V Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Ye mountains of Gilboa · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  5  Sun     V Sunday after Pentecost                   [sd]  g
           Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Ye have heard that it was said · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. If thou bring thy gift to the Altar · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. The Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  6  Mon     The Octave of Ss Peter & Paul              [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Thou art Peter · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyril and Methodius, Bishops & Confessors ("Behold an high priest")
  7  Tue     Ss. Cyril and Methodius, Bishops & Confessors [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. In holiness · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. These are · No Suff.
  8  Wed     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
  9  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 10  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           The Seven Holy Brothers, with Ss Rufina & Secunda, Martyrs (com)
           St. Joseph of Damascus (Priest) & companions, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. The Seven Holy Brothers, with Ss Rufina & Secunda, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Joseph of Damascus (Priest) & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
               Com. St Pius I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 11  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           St Pius I, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Pius I, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (VI Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. I beseech thee · Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 12  Sun     VI Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The multitude being very great · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. I have compassion on the multitude · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Anacletus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 13  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Anacletus, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Anacletus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St Anacletus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 14  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. St Henry, Emperor & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 15  Wed     St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor      [d]   w
           St Henry, Emperor & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Henry, Emperor & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Our Lady of Einsiedeln) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 16  Thu     Our Lady of Einsiedeln                     [gd]  w
           Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Alexius, Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 17  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Alexius, Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Alexius, Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 18  Sat     Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn      [d]   w
           St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot (com)
           St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (VII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Nathan the Prophet · No Suff.
               Com. Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 19  Sun     VII Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Beware of false prophets · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit · No Suff.
               Com. St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Elias the Prophet ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 20  Mon     St Elias the Prophet                       [d]   w
           St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Praxedes, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 21  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Praxedes, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Praxedes, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Mary Magdalene) · Mag. A woman in the city · No Suff.
 22  Wed     St. Mary Magdalene                         [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Mary anointed · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. A woman · No Suff.
               Com. St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St John Cassian, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 23  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           St John Cassian, Abbot (com)
           St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St John Cassian, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St John Cassian, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 24  Fri  L  Vigil of St. James                         [s]   v
           St Christina, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Christina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. James the Greater, Apostle) · Mag. For they shall deliver you · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of St. James ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St Christopher, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 25  Sat     St. James the Greater, Apostle             [2cl] r
           St Christopher, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Christopher, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be valiant · No Suff.
               Com. St Christopher, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Anne, Mother of the Blessed Virgin Mary ("While the King sitteth at his table")
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 26  Sun     St. Anne, Mother of the Blessed Virgin Mary [2cl] w
           VIII Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Give her of the fruit of her hands · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. She is beautiful · No Suff.
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 27  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Pantaleon, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Pantaleon, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St Pantaleon, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Ss. Nazarius, Celsus, Martyrs; St Victor, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nazarius, Celsus, Martyrs; St Victor, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Martha of Bethany, Virgin) · Mag. Come, Bride of Christ, and take the everlasting crown · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs ("Behold an high priest")
 29  Wed     St. Martha of Bethany, Virgin              [d]   w
           Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Come, Bride of Christ, and take the everlasting crown · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 30  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 31  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Chains of St Peter, Apostle) · Mag. For they shall deliver you · No Suff.
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Holy Maccabees, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")

AUGUST
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sat     Chains of St Peter, Apostle                [gd]  w
           Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle (com)
           Holy Maccabees, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Holy Maccabees, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (IX Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Wisdom · No Suff.
               Com. Chains of St Peter, Apostle ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. St Stephen, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  2  Sun     IX Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           St Stephen, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When the Lord was come near to Jerusalem, He… · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. It is written: My house is the house of… · No Suff.
               Com. St Stephen, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. The Finding of St. Stephen, Protomartyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  3  Mon     The Finding of St. Stephen, Protomartyr    [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If any man will come after me · No Suff.
  4  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Oswald, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  5  Wed     Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows        [gd]  w
           St Oswald, King & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Oswald, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Transfiguration of Our Lord) · Mag. While he yet spake · No Suff.
               Com. Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. Ss. Sixtus II (Pope & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  6  Thu     Transfiguration of Our Lord                [2cl] w
           Ss. Sixtus II (Pope & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And, behold, a voice out of the cloud · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sixtus II (Pope & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. While he yet spake · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sixtus II (Pope & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  7  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Donatus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Donatus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
               Com. St Donatus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  8  Sat     Vigil of St. Lawrence, Martyr              [s]   v
           Ss. Cyriacus, Largus & Smaragdus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyriacus, Largus & Smaragdus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (X Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. I dwell in the highest · Suff.
               Com. Vigil of St. Lawrence, Martyr ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St Romanus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  9  Sun     X Sunday after Pentecost                   [sd]  g
           St Romanus, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The publican, standing afar off · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Romanus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Lawrence, Martyr) · Mag. Lawrence the Deacon · No Suff.
               Com. X Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 10  Mon     St. Lawrence, Martyr                       [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Upon the bars I denied thee not, O God · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. The blessed Lawrence prayed · No Suff.
 11  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Ss Tiburtius & Susanna, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Tiburtius & Susanna, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. Ss Tiburtius & Susanna, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 12  Wed     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Maximus of Constantinople, Confessor) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 13  Thu     St Maximus of Constantinople, Confessor    [d]   w
           Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Vigil of the Assumption ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 14  Fri  L§ Vigil of the Assumption                    [s]   v
           St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of the Assumption ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 15  Sat     ASSUMPTION OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY      [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. St Joachim, Father of the B.V.M ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 16  Sun     St Joachim, Father of the B.V.M            [2cl] w
           XI Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Day II within the Octave of the Assumption (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of the Assumption ("Through thee have the gates of Paradise been thrown…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Lo, a servant of God · No Suff.
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of the Assumption ("Through thee have the gates of Paradise been thrown…")

           Octave of St Laurence (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of St Laurence ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of St Laurence ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 18  Tue     Day IV within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w

           St Helen, Empress (com)

               Com. St Helen, Empress ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. St Agapitus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Helen, Empress ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 19  Wed     Day V within the Octave of the Assumption  [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
 20  Thu     Day VI within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
 21  Fri  L  Day VII within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Assumption) · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Assumption ("Through thee have the gates of Paradise been thrown…")
               Com. Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Vigil of St. Bartholomew ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
 22  Sat     Octave Day of the Assumption               [gd]  w
           Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm (com)
           Vigil of St. Bartholomew (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Vigil of St. Bartholomew ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The holy Mother of God is exalted over choirs… · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. Vigil of St. Bartholomew ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. XII Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 23  Sun     XII Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Master, what shall I do · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Bartholomew, Apostle) · Mag. For they shall deliver you · No Suff.
               Com. XII Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 24  Mon     St. Bartholomew, Apostle                   [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be valiant · No Suff.
 25  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
 26  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Zephyrinus, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Zephyrinus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
               Com. St Zephyrinus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 27  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Fri  L  St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [gd]  w
           St Hermes, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Beheading of St. John the Baptist) · Mag. Herod sent · No Suff.
               Com. St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St Sabina, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 29  Sat     Beheading of St. John the Baptist          [gd]  r
           St Sabina, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Sabina, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XIII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. When Job heard the words · No Suff.
               Com. Beheading of St. John the Baptist ("For Herod had laid hold upon John")
               Com. Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 30  Sun     XIII Sunday after Pentecost                [sd]  g
           Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. As Jesus passed through · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. And one of them · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
 31  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")

SEPTEMBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Giles, Abbot (com)
           Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Giles, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Giles, Abbot ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
               Com. Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  2  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor ("Lord, Thou deliverest unto me five talents")
  3  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Gorazde of Prague, Bishop & Martyr) · Mag. This man is holy · No Suff.
  4  Fri  L  St Gorazde of Prague, Bishop & Martyr      [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If any man will come after me · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
  5  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XIV Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. In all these things Job sinned not · Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
  6  Sun     XIV Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Take no thought, saying: What shall we eat? · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. Seek ye first the kingdom of God · Suff.
  7  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. Let us keep with rejoicing the Birthday of the… · No Suff.
               Com. St Hadrian, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  8  Tue     Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary        [2cl] w
           St Hadrian, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Let us this day keep solemnly · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hadrian, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Let us keep with rejoicing the Birthday of the… · No Suff.
               Com. St Hadrian, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  9  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Gorgonius, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gorgonius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
               Com. St Gorgonius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 10  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 11  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss Protus & Hyacinth, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Protus & Hyacinth, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (The Most Holy Name of Mary) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
 12  Sat     The Most Holy Name of Mary                 [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XV Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Remember not, Lord · No Suff.
               Com. The Most Holy Name of Mary ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 13  Sun     XV Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Jesus went into a city called Nain · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. A great prophet is risen up among us · No Suff.
               Com. Exaltation of the Holy Cross ("Thou art higher")
 14  Mon     Exaltation of the Holy Cross               [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thou art higher · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Seven Sorrows of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Exaltation of the Holy Cross ("Thou art higher")
               Com. St Nicomedes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 15  Tue     Seven Sorrows of the B.V.M                 [2cl] w
           St Nicomedes, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Nicomedes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Nicomedes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Ss. Cornelius (Pope) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. September Ember Wednesday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 16  Wed  L§ Ss. Cornelius (Pope) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs [d]   r
           Ss Euphemia, Lucy & Geminian, Martyrs (com)
           September Ember Wednesday (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Euphemia, Lucy & Geminian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. September Ember Wednesday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. In heaven do rejoice the souls of the Saints · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Euphemia, Lucy & Geminian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. September Ember Wednesday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 17  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 18  Fri  L§ September Ember Friday                     [sd]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Januarius & companions, Martyrs) · Mag. For theirs is the Kingdom of heaven · No Suff.
               Com. September Ember Friday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
               Com. St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. September Ember Saturday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 19  Sat  L§ St Januarius & companions, Martyrs         [d]   r
           St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           September Ember Saturday (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. September Ember Saturday ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVI Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. O Adonai, O Lord God · No Suff.
               Com. St Januarius & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Eustace & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 20  Sun     XVI Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           St Eustace & companions, Martyrs (d)
           Lauds   g · Ben. As Jesus went into the house · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Eustace & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist) · Mag. For they shall deliver you · No Suff.
               Com. XVI Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 21  Mon     St. Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist        [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be valiant · No Suff.
 22  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Maurice & companions, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Maurice & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Maurice & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 23  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           St. Linus, Pope & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St. Linus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
               Com. St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St. Linus, Pope & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 24  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 25  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 26  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. O my Lord, the King Almighty · Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 27  Sun     XVII Sunday after Pentecost                [sd]  g
           Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Master, which is the great commandment · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. What think ye of Christ? Whose Son is He? · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Dedication of St. Michael the Archangel) · Mag. Great Michael · No Suff.
 29  Tue     DEDICATION OF ST. MICHAEL THE ARCHANGEL    [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The sea was moved · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Great Michael · No Suff.
               Com. St Jerome, Priest, Confessor & Doctor ("Behold an high priest")
 30  Wed     St Jerome, Priest, Confessor & Doctor      [gd]  w
           St Gregory of Armenia, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Armenia, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O right excellent Teacher · No Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Armenia, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
               Com. St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")

OCTOBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Holy Guardian Angels) · Mag. Are they not all · No Suff.
  2  Fri  L  Holy Guardian Angels                       [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Angel that talked · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Praise ye God · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
  3  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVIII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. The Lord open your hearts · Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
  4  Sun     XVIII Sunday after Pentecost               [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord said unto the sick · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. And immediately, he that had been sick of the… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Placidus & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  5  Mon     St. Placidus & companions, Martyrs         [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. In heaven do rejoice the souls of the Saints · No Suff.
  6  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (The Holy Rosary of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  7  Wed     The Holy Rosary of the Blessed Virgin Mary [gd]  w
           Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs (com)
           St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold an high priest")
  8  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
  9  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss. Denys, Rusticus & Eleutherius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Denys, Rusticus & Eleutherius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
 10  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Motherhood of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday Office of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
 11  Sun     Motherhood of the B.V.M                    [2cl] w
           XIX Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("Pray for us, O holy N")

           St Wilfrid, Bishop & Confessor (com)
